workflow.purpose:
Orchestrate living knowledge documentation: analyze what was built (story mode) or what exists (file mode), then create or update topic-scoped documents in `.docs/wiki/subsystems/[subsystem-name]/`.
Produces documents across five categories: - systems/ — WHAT exists (domain subsystems and their current state) - patterns/ — HOW things work (reusable implementation patterns) - cross-cutting/ — concerns spanning multiple systems - guides/ — step-by-step recipes for common implementation tasks - decisions/ — WHY significant choices were made (ADRs)
Two input modes that converge into ONE execution flow: - **Story mode** (user-invoked): Analyzes git changes after story implementation, detects affected subsystem(s), spawns one agent per subsystem. - **File mode** (invoked by map-subsystem step 8.7): Receives a file list for one module in one subsystem, spawns one agent for that subsystem.
Both modes produce the same output: `{ subsystem: files }` map fed to per-subsystem agents. The agents are identical regardless of mode — they read all source files, read ALL existing wiki docs, and make thorough create-vs-update decisions.
workflow.mandatory-context: The orchestrator reads ONLY this workflow — it stays lightweight. Each sub-agent reads the five document templates itself before writing. Also read any parameters passed as $ARGUMENTS in the invoking command.

process.step:
Parse $ARGUMENTS to determine execution mode:
Path to story artifacts folder Number of recent commits OR comma-separated SHAs
Comma-separated source file paths Human-readable module name Subsystem this module belongs to Comma-separated file paths and/or directory paths to pre-existing documentation Path to a pre-built inventory file (existing-docs-inventory.md) that indexes a large doc directory. When provided, the curation agent reads this inventory instead of re-scanning the directory.
Display stage banner:
``` ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ACE > Map Story > [story mode | file mode] │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ```
Continue to step 2.

``` subsystem_work = { "[subsystem-name]": { "files": [list of source file paths], "file_statuses": { "path": "A|M|D" }, // story mode only "story_summary": "...", // story mode only "module_name": "...", // file mode only "existing_docs": [...] // file mode only, if provided } } ```
Collect changed file names and statuses.
Determine the diff range:
```bash git log --name-status -n [N] git diff --name-status HEAD~[N]..HEAD ```
```bash git log --name-status [first-sha]^..[last-sha] git diff --name-status [first-sha]^..[last-sha] ```
```bash git diff --name-status git diff --name-status --cached ```
Collect every changed file with its status (A = added, M = modified, D = deleted).
Map each changed file path to its subsystem:
Build one entry per subsystem in `subsystem_work`.
Read story context (if provided) and produce a brief summary.
Read all markdown files in the story artifacts folder Extract a summary (10-15 lines max): what the story intended, key acceptance criteria, significant decisions mentioned Attach the same summary to every subsystem entry (shared intent context)
NOTE: The implementation may deviate from the plan — agents document what WAS built, not what was planned.
Set story_summary = "No story artifacts. Document from code changes."
Display to user: ``` Affected subsystems: - [subsystem] ([N] files changed) - [subsystem] ([M] files changed) ```
Resolve existing-docs entries.
Split the `existing-docs` parameter into individual entries. Classify each entry:
Keep as-is (already curated by the caller) Needs curation (may contain hundreds of irrelevant files)
Spawn a curation agent to select only relevant docs. If `existing-docs-inventory` was provided (not `none`), pass it to the agent so it reads the pre-built inventory instead of re-scanning the directory.
``` Task( prompt="You are a documentation curation agent. Your job is to select ONLY the documentation files relevant to a specific code module from a large doc directory.
**Module name:** [module-name param] **Source files in this module:** [files param] **Documentation directory:** [directory path] **Pre-built inventory (if available):** [existing-docs-inventory path, or 'none']
Process:
IF a pre-built inventory file was provided (not 'none'): 1. Read the inventory file — it contains file paths, titles, and summaries for every doc in the directory, already indexed 2. Compare each inventory entry against the module's source files and name 3. Select ONLY entries whose subject matter is relevant to this module 4. Return the file paths from the selected inventory entries (Do NOT re-scan the directory — the inventory is the source of truth)
IF no inventory file is available: 1. Recursively list ALL files in the documentation directory down to every leaf subdirectory. Use Glob(pattern='**/*', path='[directory path]') to ensure no nesting level is missed. Do NOT use shallow ls or single-level globs. 2. For each file, read its title and first 10-20 lines to understand its subject 3. Compare against the module's source files and module name 4. Select ONLY files whose subject matter is relevant to this module
Relevance criteria (both paths): - Documents describing the same domain concepts - Architecture docs covering systems these files participate in - API docs for interfaces these files implement or consume - Decision records about choices visible in this code Be SELECTIVE — fewer relevant docs is better than many tangential ones.
Return ONLY a comma-separated list of relevant file paths (absolute or relative). If no docs are relevant, return 'none'. Do NOT return explanations — just the file list.", subagent_type="ace-wiki-mapper", description="Curate relevant docs for [module-name]" ) ```
Replace the directory entry with the curated file list returned by the agent. Merge with any file-path entries that were already curated. The final `existing_docs` is a flat list of relevant file paths only.
Display curated docs to user:
``` ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Curated Existing Docs for [module-name] │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Directory scanned: [directory path] Relevant docs selected ([N] of [total] files):
- [file path 1] - [file path 2] - ...
[If none selected: "No relevant docs found in directory."] ```
Skip curation — use them directly.
Build the work map:
``` subsystem_work = { "[subsystem-name param]": { "files": [split files param into list], "module_name": "[module-name param]", "existing_docs": [resolved flat list of curated file paths, or empty] } } ```
Continue to step 3.

Run agent in foreground. Run ALL agents with `run_in_background=true`.
For each entry in `subsystem_work`, spawn one ace-wiki-mapper agent.
Each agent receives a self-contained prompt. The prompt MUST include all the instructions below — the agent has no other source of truth for how to work.
``` Task( prompt="You are creating or updating living knowledge documentation for the [subsystem_name] subsystem.
TEMPLATES — Read ALL of these before writing ANY docs:
- System docs: .claude/templates/system.xml - Pattern docs: .claude/templates/pattern.xml - Cross-cutting docs: .claude/templates/system-cross-cutting.xml - Guide docs: .claude/templates/guide.xml - Decision docs: .claude/templates/decizions.xml
YOUR INPUTS:
**Subsystem:** [subsystem_name] **Wiki directory:** .docs/wiki/subsystems/[subsystem_name]/
**Source files to analyze:** [list every file path with its A/M/D status (story mode) or just paths (file mode)]
**Story context:** [story_summary from step 2c]
**Module name:** [module_name] **Pre-existing documentation:** [existing_docs paths, or 'none']
Read EVERY source file listed above (skip deleted files — note them for removal from existing docs). For each file, understand:
Class hierarchies and interface implementations
Function signatures and contracts
State management (Redux slices, local state, database)
Constants, enums, and configuration
Integration points (imports from other systems, event handling)
Error handling patterns
DI registrations, factory/registry entries
THE ACTUAL CODE IS THE SOURCE OF TRUTH. Story docs and existing docs are context for intent — the code is what gets documented.
Also read the pre-existing documentation files. These contain prior knowledge about intent, decisions, and history. Use them to understand WHY things were built a certain way, but the source code always wins when there is a conflict.
READ ALL EXISTING WIKI DOCS (MANDATORY — NO SHORTCUTS)
Before writing ANYTHING, you MUST read ALL existing wiki documents for this subsystem. Not titles. Not summaries. THE FULL CONTENT of every single document.
Scan for existing docs — use the Glob tool (NOT bash find): ``` Glob(pattern='**/*.md', path='.docs/wiki/subsystems/[subsystem_name]') ```
Read EVERY document found — fully:
systems/*.md — every system document, complete
patterns/*.md — every pattern document, complete
cross-cutting/*.md — every cross-cutting document, complete
guides/*.md — every guide document, complete
decisions/*.md — every ADR, complete
Build a mental map of what is ALREADY documented:
Which systems already have docs? What sections do they cover?
Which patterns are already documented? What implementations are listed?
Which cross-cutting concerns are documented? What registrations are listed?
Which guides exist? Are the steps still accurate?
Which decisions are recorded?
This map is your ANTI-DUPLICATION defense. You cannot avoid creating duplicate content if you have not read what already exists.
If the wiki directory does not exist: ```bash mkdir -p .docs/wiki/subsystems/[subsystem_name]/{systems,patterns,cross-cutting,guides,decisions} ```
ANALYZE AND CATEGORIZE
For each significant component/change in the source files, determine which doc category it affects:
CREATE vs UPDATE — the critical decision.
For EACH item you want to document, check your mental map from Step B:
UPDATE that doc. Add/expand sections. Do NOT create a new doc. CREATE a new doc. But ONLY if this is a genuinely new topic.
Add the section there. Do NOT create a separate doc.
Merge into the existing doc. NEVER create parallel docs about the same concern.
NEVER create a story-scoped or module-scoped document. All docs are TOPIC-scoped — about the concern, not about the story or module.
When updating: ADD new sections or expand existing ones. Do NOT rewrite sections that have not changed. UPDATE file trees to reflect current state. REMOVE references to deleted files or components.
WRITE DOCUMENTATION
For each item in your action plan:
Read the appropriate template XML (from the list above). Follow the template structure, fill all relevant sections, omit sections that genuinely don't apply. Read the existing doc, add/expand sections, preserve unchanged content, match existing style.
DIAGRAM RULE — ALL visual representations of architecture, dependencies, flows, or relationships MUST use ```mermaid fenced code blocks. This means: NO ASCII arrows (->), NO tree structures for dependencies, NO PlantUML, NO custom notation. If you would write `A -> B -> C`, write a mermaid flowchart instead. The ONLY exception for ASCII is file trees (directory listings).
E2E FLOW RULE — Every systems/*.md doc MUST have at least one ```mermaid sequenceDiagram. This is non-negotiable. Trace the complete E2E flow from entry point through all layers to data store and back. If you cannot trace it, read more source files until you can.
Documentation style (ALL docs):
EXTREMELY SUCCINCT — every word must add value
NO FLUFF — direct, actionable information only
Bullet points over paragraphs
File trees: ASCII only (|-- and backtick-dash-dash) — NEVER Unicode box-drawing
Diagrams (sequence, class, boundary): MUST be ```mermaid fenced blocks
Code references: `file-path:ClassName.methodName` or `file-path:functionName` NEVER use line numbers (they go stale with every edit)
Inline snippets ONLY for interfaces, types, and contracts that define API shape
When referencing a class/module: `file-path:ClassName`
When referencing a method: `file-path:ClassName.methodName`
When referencing a function: `file-path:functionName`
When referencing a type/interface: `file-path:InterfaceName`
Cross-reference related docs with markdown links
What NOT to document:
Story numbers, sprint context, or agile artifacts Planned vs Actual comparisons Acceptance criteria checklists Revision history (git handles this) Duplicated implementation code (reference it, don't copy it) Line numbers in references (they go stale) Testing docs, coverage metrics, test code Performance benchmarks Debugging utilities or monitoring code ASCII art for diagrams (ALL diagrams MUST be ```mermaid blocks; exception: file trees use ASCII)
QUALITY VERIFICATION
After writing all docs, verify each one:
File exists and has substantial content (>10 lines for new docs) Code references use `file:Symbol` format — no line numbers No document is story-scoped or module-scoped in its title or structure Related systems/patterns/cross-cutting docs are cross-referenced with links File trees use ASCII only — no Unicode box-drawing characters Constants and enums locations are documented (agents must never hardcode) No content duplicated from another existing doc in this subsystem Every doc answers ONE clear question about how the system works
YOUR REPORT (return this)
Return a structured report:
Subsystem: [subsystem_name]
Documents created: - [path] ([line count] lines) — [what this doc covers]
Documents updated: - [path] — [what sections were added/changed]
Documents unchanged: - [path] — [why no change was needed]
Issues found (if any): - [description of any problems encountered]
",
subagent_type="ace-wiki-mapper", description="Document [subsystem_name] subsystem" ) ```
Continue to step 4.

If agents were spawned in background, wait for all to complete. Read each agent's report.
If any agent failed:
Note the failure and the subsystem affected Do NOT re-attempt — inform the user and let them decide
Merge all agent reports into a combined result.
Continue to step 5.

Security scan across all affected subsystems — use the Grep tool (NOT bash grep):
``` Grep( pattern="(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|sk_live_|sk_test_|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}|-----BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY)", path=".docs/wiki/subsystems/", output_mode="content" ) ```
SECRETS_FOUND — pause and alert user. CLEAN.
Display the combined report:
``` ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ACE > Map Story > Complete │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
## Documentation Update Report
**Story**: [story name from artifacts, or "Git changes"] **Affected Subsystems**: [list]
[For each subsystem, include the agent's report:]
### [subsystem_name]
Documents created: - [path] ([line count] lines) — [description]
Documents updated: - [path] — [what changed]
[If any agent failures:] ### Failures - [subsystem]: [error]
Next > `/clear` first for a fresh context window, then:
/ace:map-story — document another story /ace:map-subsystem [subsystem] — map or refresh an entire subsystem Review files in .docs/wiki/subsystems/ ```
Return the agent's report directly to the calling workflow (map-subsystem step 8.7). Do NOT display banners or next steps — the calling workflow handles presentation.
End workflow.

success_criteria.criterion: subsystem_work map built correctly from either mode's inputs
One agent spawned per subsystem (parallel when 2+)
Each agent reads ALL source files for its subsystem (not just names)
Each agent reads ALL existing wiki docs for its subsystem FULLY (not summaries, not titles — complete content)
Each agent checks every existing doc before deciding create vs update
No duplicate documentation created — agent merges into existing docs when topic overlaps
No story-scoped or module-scoped documents created (all topic-scoped)
Each document follows its template structure from the XML templates
Updates ADD to existing docs rather than rewriting unchanged sections
Code references use `file:Symbol` format (not line numbers)
File trees use ASCII only (no Unicode box-drawing)
Every systems/*.md doc has at least one mermaid sequenceDiagram (E2E flow is mandatory)
Inline code only for interface/type contracts
Related systems cross-referenced with markdown links
Security scan passed (no secrets in generated docs)
Clear structured report from each agent, merged into final report
